_comment: research.example.json — synthetic placeholder for the gitignored research.db SQLite database. The real DB is gitignored under the *.db pattern. This .example.json captures the schema + 3 sample rows so a reviewer can rebuild a fresh DB on clone.
_rebuild_recipe: From this file, recreate research.db with: python3 -c "import json,sqlite3; data=json.load(open('research.example.json')); conn=sqlite3.connect('research.db'); cur=conn.cursor(); [cur.execute(s) for s in data['schema']['statements']]; [cur.execute(f\"INSERT INTO {t} ({','.join(rows[0].keys())}) VALUES ({','.join('?'*len(rows[0]))})\", tuple(r.values())) for t,rows in data['rows'].items() for r in rows]; conn.commit()"

statements: CREATE TABLE research_entries (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, slug TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, title TEXT, file_path TEXT NOT NULL, file_format TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT, updated_at TEXT, complexity_score REAL, effort_tier TEXT, confidence REAL, base_hours INTEGER, business_process TEXT, research_type TEXT, generated BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0)
CREATE TABLE integrations (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT NOT NULL, research_id INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY(research_id) REFERENCES research_entries(id) ON DELETE CASCADE)
